text.title: Prompt Engineering — Reasoning Patterns — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the prompt-engineering-reasoning methodology. Sources: OpenAI o1 / o3 reasoning model docs; Anthropic extended-thinking documentation; Google Gemini thinking models docs.

rule.statement: On reasoning-class models (o1, Claude extended thinking, Gemini Thinking) state the goal and constraints, then stop; do not paste 'think step by step' instructions.

rationale.source: OpenAI o1 / o3 reasoning model docs

rule.rationale: Reasoning models reason internally; verbose CoT instructions hurt rather than help and inflate latency 2–10×.

rule.statement: Use the explicit step budget controls when available (`max_completion_tokens` for o1, `max_thinking_tokens` for Claude); never let thinking run unbounded.
rule.rationale: Unbounded thinking is the dominant cost-blowup vector on reasoning models; budgets are the only mechanical cap.

rule.statement: State the exact target output shape early (JSON schema, regex); reasoning models follow shape instructions extremely well.
rule.rationale: Reasoning models reward explicit shape requests; shape-first prompts consistently win shape-last variants on benchmarks.

rule.statement: When the model exposes a reasoning trace separately (e.g. extended thinking), do not include the trace in downstream context.
rule.rationale: Reasoning traces leak chain-of-thought and inflate downstream context for no quality gain.

rule.statement: Score reasoning models on task success, not style; they may emit different phrasing than non-reasoning families for equivalent answers.

rationale.source: OpenAI o1 / o3 reasoning model docs

rule.rationale: Style-based eval rejects correct answers from reasoning models, creating the false signal that they 'don't work'.
